title: Review of Bruce Lee Grave Site, Seattle, WA
content: The church was magnificently decorated; on the somber draped catafalque in a casket in the form of a canoe rested all that was mortal of Princess Angeline. Date

title: Princess Angeline - Pacific Northwest Royalty - bend branches
content: Princess Angeline by Edward S. Did you know that a princess is buried at Lake View Cemetery in Seattle? The woman known as “Princess Angeline” was the daughter of Chief Sealth, aka Chief Seattle or Chief Si’ahl. She was the first Native American that he photographed. Princess Angeline's gravestone with Yesler memorial in the background 8July2016. I recently attended the funeral of a close relative at Lake View Cemetery and found Princess Angeline’s gravestone nearby. There was a magnificent funeral for her in 1896 at Seattle’s Church of Our Lady of Good Help. It said that she “was a life long supporter of the white settlers” and that she had been converted to Christianity and was named by Mrs. D. Chief Sealth befriended many settlers and the city was named “Seattle” in his honor. Her friend Catherine Maynard, wife of Doc Maynard, thought she deserved a name that would help people recognize her importance as the daughter of the city’s namesake.

title: Kikisoblu Kick-is-om-lo Wewick “Princess Angeline”...
content: Princess Angeline, upon her death, was given a decent funeral in Seattle's Church of Our Lady of Good Help. Her coffin was in the shape of a canoe. May 29, 1896

title: Princess Angeline grave
content: Among those buried in Lake View Cemetery is Princess Angeline, the eldest daughter of Chief Sealth who gave his name to Seattle.

title: Princess Angeline's burial at Lake View Cemetery in Seattle
content: People observing the strange old lady have reported that she vanished before the ferry reached Bainbridge Island. And they never saw her walk
